# Source Card: Outcomes for patients with severe chronic neutropenia treated with granulocyte colony-stimulating factor

## Summary
According to PubMed, this SCNIR (Severe Chronic Neutropenia International Registry) report (Dale et al., Blood Advances 2022, [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1182/bloodadvances.2021005684)) establishes the operational definition of severe chronic neutropenia (ANC <0.5 x 10^9/L for >3 months) and the sub-classification cutoffs for cyclic and congenital neutropenia, drawn from more than 16,000 patient-years of prospective observation. For a pediatric CDS module it supplies (a) neutropenia-severity thresholds and (b) etiology-based malignancy-risk stratification: myeloid malignancy (MDS/AML) is concentrated in congenital neutropenia (11.3%) and was never observed in the autoimmune/idiopathic group (0/816), which carries a favorable prognosis. These are registry-derived definitions and outcomes, not a formal age-banded reference-interval study, and must be treated as implementation inputs, not diagnosis/treatment directives.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) SCN defined as neutrophils <0.5 x 10^9/L for >3 months — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:7fb82392dd4f00e8727805747bbb9609fc5431d9a8f74f88e0275792ba2bf310]"
- (ev_002) Enrollment operational criterion: ANC <0.5 x 10^9/L on >=3 occasions over >=3 months — locator: Methods > Enrollment criteria — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:8b839c90f5ca899edaed8e7447d14c9dece5e310bc82ccc6da707cc9c3e2c024]"
- (ev_003) Cyclic neutropenia: nadir <0.2, peak usually >1.0 x 10^9/L, ~3-week cycle — locator: Methods > Enrollment criteria — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:7e4eebc0a49f4962a1d6b9f3fca45923a77b7e9768c8dddb4b186777d10951ab]"
- (ev_004) Congenital neutropenia: ANC <0.5 x 10^9/L persisting beyond age 0.25-1.0 y — locator: Methods > Enrollment criteria — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:ff2928acf51095774b2b11c1dd3dd26296b7aed50074c54ff3087d3304b1c9a4]"
- (ev_005) Myeloid malignancy 4.4% overall, 11.3% congenital — locator: Results > Myeloid malignancies / Table 6 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:63ac429aebb102e07036be7845a73bef614770d0ba2eec04024d21823b9d69bb]"
- (ev_006) Zero myeloid malignancy in autoimmune/idiopathic (0/816) — locator: Results > Lymphoid malignancies — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:c98dbc0ff8a7f342f1fecc85f8e50c148c3f5feda65a1c98c06453e96c87daa5]"
- (ev_007) Lymphoid malignancy 1.5% (12/816) in autoimmune/idiopathic, median age 46.9 y — locator: Results > Lymphoid malignancies — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:9cca0c52b88175081dd8328df08c38b476bd1653e7f3f9b10ac09aaf643807ce]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: registry cohort of 1752 patients across congenital (670), cyclic (266), and autoimmune/idiopathic (816) etiologies; both pediatric (<18 y) and adult (>=18 y) cohorts. No sex-specific or gestational/postnatal-age-specific ANC cutoffs are provided.
- Assay/method: ANC derived from CBC differential; the specific analyzer or manual-vs-automated method is NOT stated. At the very low counts defining SCN, ANC is generally method-dependent (automated differentials unreliable at low WBC) — the source does not address this, so it is a known gap, not a source claim.
- Lifecycle: G-CSF approved by FDA/EMA in 1994; SCNIR enrollment May 1994-June 2020. Definitions are SCNIR registry enrollment criteria (Blood Adv 2022 report). Not superseded by a newer version within this source.
- Rendering caveat: the PMC HTML full text dropped superscript exponents (rendered "× 10/L"); the correct unit throughout is × 10^9/L, confirmed by the abstract's "× 109/L" and by the publisher/JATS full text (EuropePMC PMC9278291 fullTextXML, which renders "10<sup>9</sup>/L" in every enrollment-criteria sentence). The ev_002/ev_003/ev_004 enrollment-criteria quotes have been restored to the source's true unit (× 10^9/L, superscript transcribed as ^9) so the verbatim carries the asserted exponent; ev_001 retains the abstract's "× 109/L" form. UCUM units recorded as 10*9/L.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Etiology-based risk stratification is directly referral-relevant: congenital neutropenia carries substantial myeloid-malignancy risk (11.3%) and is the phenotype where MDS/AML transformation concentrates, whereas autoimmune/idiopathic neutropenia had zero myeloid transformation (0/816) and a favorable prognosis. A CDS rule may use "congenital pattern / persistent <0.5 x 10^9/L beyond infancy" as a specialist-referral / hematology-workup flag. The source frames this as prognosis, NOT as a diagnostic or treatment directive.
- Cyclic pattern (nadir <0.2 rising to >1.0 x 10^9/L on ~21-day periodicity) is a distinct scope-boundary signal requiring serial counts to detect.

## Limitations & conflicts
- No age-band, sex, or gestational/postnatal-age reference intervals: this source gives disease-definition cutoffs, not population reference ranges (e.g., neonatal ANC references would come from a CALIPER-type study, absent here). Do NOT infer age-banded normals from this source.
- Sex-specific thresholds: not locatable in this source — record as GAP (no sex-stratified ANC cutoff in Dale et al. 2022).
- Assay/analyzer dependence of low ANC: not addressed in source — GAP.
- No conflict with other sources is recorded here; the < 0.5 x 10^9/L SCN definition is consistent with the abstract and standard hematology usage. Any conflicting neonatal/age-specific ANC cutoffs from other cards must be PRESERVED as separate population-specific values, never averaged against this disease-definition cutoff.
- Directive boundary: nothing in this card authorizes diagnosis, dosing, or treatment. G-CSF dose figures in the source (Table 3) are descriptive registry observations and are deliberately NOT extracted as CDS thresholds.
